summary: This cluster investigates the molecular physiology of dinoflagellates, specifically through the lens of their ion channel and signaling repertoire. Using transcriptomic analysis of 27 samples, researchers reconstructed a consensus "channelome" comprising 36 ion channel families, which included homologs of animal and plant proteins as well as unique protist-specific channels. The findings emphasized the organisms' physiological adaptability, driven by extensive G-protein coupled receptor and calcium-dependent signaling pathways. The work is primarily bioinformatic and comparative in scope, focusing on the evolutionary and functional diversity of dinoflagellate signaling without a specific geographic or ecological focus.
